data.title: De Beers Implements Significant Reductions Amid Diamond Industry Crisis
data.description: De Beers has significantly reduced its group of selected diamond buyers in response to an ongoing industry crisis, according to sources familiar with the situation. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the challenges faced by the former monopoly as it navigates a prolonged downturn in the diamond market. The move reflects the company's efforts to adapt to changing market conditions and stabilize its operations amid declining demand and pricing pressures. The diamond industry has been experiencing turbulence, prompting De Beers to reassess its strategies and buyer relationships. This decision marks one of the most substantial adjustments in the company's history, underscoring the severity of the current market challenges.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Ecolab Acquires CoolIT for $4.75 Billion to Enhance AI Data Center Cooling
data.description: Ecolab has announced its acquisition of CoolIT, a company specializing in cooling technology for AI data centers, in a deal valued at $4.75 billion. Bloomberg posted on X that this all-cash transaction aims to bolster Ecolab's capabilities in providing advanced cooling solutions for the rapidly growing AI sector. The acquisition reflects Ecolab's strategic move to expand its footprint in the technology infrastructure market, particularly as demand for efficient cooling systems in AI data centers continues to rise. This deal underscores the increasing importance of sustainable and effective cooling technologies in supporting the operational needs of AI-driven facilities.

data.title: Vanity Fair Feature Highlights Crypto's Image Challenges
data.description: A Vanity Fair article published on March 17 has stirred controversy following comments from Hayden Adams, who revealed he declined a photo shoot request to pose in a bathrobe in a sauna. According to NS3.AI, the incident underscores a disconnect between the institutional growth of the cryptocurrency sector and its ongoing struggle with public perception.

data.title: AlphaTON Capital acquires a 60% controlling stake in GAMEE for up to $11 million.
data.description: Nasdaq-listed AlphaTON Capital, the financial arm of TON (Total Non-Total Revenue) companies, announced a definitive agreement to acquire a 60% controlling stake in gaming platform GAMEE for up to $11 million, including a two-year, performance-linked additional payment. GAMEE, a wholly owned subsidiary of Animoca Brands, is valued at $18 million in the transaction. Under the terms of the agreement, AlphaTON Capital will take over the day-to-day operations of GAMEE and formally establish a strategic alliance with Animoca Brands, while prohibiting Animoca Brands from acquiring a controlling stake in AlphaTON Capital within two years. (Globenewswire)

data.title: Neutrl has completed its DNS migration and resumed operation. The official announcement states that the original domain name will be gradually decommissioned.
data.description: In response to the recent DNS hijacking incident, the DeFi protocol Neutrl has migrated its official domain to a new address and switched to a new DNS service provider. The smart contracts have been unsuspended and are now running normally. Neutrl further stated that user funds are safe, as all protocol reserve assets are stored in custodial wallets and isolated through a custody framework and over-the-counter (OES) settlement mechanism. The official warning is that users should not access the original domain, which will be gradually deactivated. Users who have interacted with the affected domain are advised to revoke related authorizations, including Permit2 authorizations to specified malicious addresses, and to clean up all unknown authorizations.

data.title: Israeli Weapons Factory in Czech Republic Set Ablaze, Al Jazeera Reports
data.description: An Israeli weapons factory located in the Czech Republic has been set on fire, according to Al Jazeera. The incident has raised concerns about the security of international defense facilities. Authorities are currently investigating the cause of the fire and assessing the extent of the damage. The factory is known for producing advanced military equipment, and the fire could potentially impact production schedules and supply chains. Further details about the incident and its implications are expected to emerge as the investigation progresses.

data.title: Paris Transforms Under Mayor Hidalgo's Car-Free Initiative
data.description: Paris has undergone significant changes under the leadership of outgoing Mayor Anne Hidalgo, who advocated for a car-free agenda. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the city's transformation into a greener and cleaner environment, enhancing its appeal for walking and biking. Hidalgo's policies have reshaped the urban landscape, prioritizing pedestrian-friendly spaces and reducing vehicular traffic. Her tenure has left a lasting impact on the city's infrastructure and lifestyle, promoting sustainable urban living.

data.title: Federal Reserve Governor Bowman: Still expects three rate cuts in 2026
data.description: Federal Reserve Governor Bowman stated that he still expects three interest rate cuts in 2026. (Jinshi)

data.title: Eightco invests an additional $40 million in OpenAI, bringing its total investment to $90 million.
data.description: Nasdaq-listed Eightco Holdings announced an additional $40 million investment in OpenAI, bringing its total investment to $90 million. In addition to its OpenAI stake, Eightco Holdings holds 277,222,975 WLD, 11,068 ETH, and $76 million in cash and stablecoins. ORBS, holding nearly 10% of the circulating supply of WLD, making it the largest public market participant in the Worldcoin ecosystem, recently announced $125 million in investment commitments. Ethereum treasury company Bitmine led the investment with $75 million, and ARK Invest committed at least $25 million, appointing Bitmine Chairman Tom Lee to its board of directors. (PRNewswire)

data.title: The White House releases national artificial intelligence legislative framework.
data.description: According to market sources, the White House has released a national framework for artificial intelligence legislation. (Jinshi)

data.title: Guizhou police cracked a USDT fraud case: the suspect used a "read-only wallet" to steal 7,118 USDT.
data.description: Police in Wanshan District, Tongren City, Guizhou Province, China, recently cracked a cryptocurrency trading fraud case. The victim, Chen, was swindled out of 7,118 USDT (equivalent to over 50,000 yuan) by a scammer named Tan when trading USDT offline. Tan used a "read-only wallet" (an address that can only be used by the user to receive and view funds) and a cash display scam. Investigation revealed that Tan first displayed cash to gain Chen's trust, then immediately transferred the USDT to the "read-only wallet" and denied the fraud. The case is currently under further investigation. Police remind the public that cryptocurrency-related businesses are illegal financial activities with extremely high risks; offline cash displays may be a scam; always verify the authenticity of the wallet address before transferring funds. (Guangming.com)

data.title: Chinese Carmakers Experience Surge in Interest
data.description: Chinese car manufacturers are witnessing a significant increase in interest. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the growing attention these companies are receiving. This surge is attributed to various factors, including advancements in technology and competitive pricing strategies. The increased interest is expected to bolster the market presence of Chinese carmakers globally, as they continue to innovate and expand their offerings.

data.title: Ledger Appoints New CFO Amid U.S. Expansion Plans
data.description: Ledger has announced the appointment of John Andrews, a former executive at Circle, as its new chief financial officer. According to NS3.AI, this move is part of Ledger's strategic plan to expand its operations in the United States. The company also intends to establish a new office in New York to support its growth initiatives.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Unitree Robotics Advances Toward Shanghai IPO
data.description: China's prominent robotics firm, Unitree Robotics, has taken a significant step towards a domestic listing by filing for an initial public offering (IPO) in Shanghai. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the growing investor interest in the robotics sector. The move underscores the increasing focus on technological advancements within China, as companies seek to capitalize on the burgeoning demand for robotics and automation solutions. Unitree Robotics, known for its innovative products, aims to leverage this IPO to expand its market presence and enhance its research and development capabilities. The filing marks a pivotal moment for the company as it navigates the competitive landscape of the robotics industry.

data.title: UK Regulators to Investigate Bankrupt Mortgage Firm MFS
data.description: UK regulators have announced an investigation into the bankrupt mortgage firm MFS. According to Jin10, the inquiry aims to uncover the reasons behind the company's financial collapse and assess its impact on the broader financial sector. The investigation will focus on the firm's business practices and compliance with regulatory standards. Authorities are particularly interested in understanding the circumstances that led to MFS's insolvency and the potential repercussions for investors and clients. The findings could lead to further regulatory actions or reforms within the mortgage industry. The investigation underscores the importance of maintaining transparency and accountability in financial operations to protect stakeholders and ensure market stability.

data.title: Fed Governor Bowman: Too Early to Assess Iran War's Impact on Fed
data.description: Federal Reserve Governor Michelle Bowman stated that it is premature to determine the implications of the ongoing conflict in Iran for the Federal Reserve's policies. According to Jin10, Bowman emphasized the uncertainty surrounding the situation and its potential effects on the U.S. economy. She noted that the Fed is closely monitoring developments and will assess any economic impacts as more information becomes available. Bowman highlighted the importance of remaining vigilant and adaptable in response to global geopolitical events that could influence economic stability. The Federal Reserve continues to focus on its dual mandate of promoting maximum employment and maintaining price stability, while considering external factors that may affect its policy decisions.

data.title: Goldman Sachs CEO Solomon Anticipates Growth in Wealth Management and Alternatives
data.description: Goldman Sachs CEO David Solomon is optimistic about the firm's ability to surpass its return targets through a resurgence in deal-making and expansion in its wealth management and alternatives business. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Solomon's strategic focus on these areas as key drivers for future growth. The CEO's confidence stems from anticipated improvements in market conditions, which he believes will bolster the firm's performance. Solomon's strategy reflects a broader industry trend towards diversifying revenue streams and enhancing client offerings in response to evolving financial landscapes.

data.title: Federal Reserve Governor Bowman Emphasizes Regulatory Reforms for U.S. Banks
data.description: Federal Reserve Governor Michelle Bowman has expressed her desire to ensure that regulatory reforms are focused on the needs of U.S. banks. According to Jin10, Bowman highlighted the importance of tailoring regulations to address the specific requirements of American financial institutions. She emphasized that reforms should not only enhance the stability of the banking system but also support the unique operational needs of U.S. banks. Bowman's comments come amid ongoing discussions about the future of banking regulations and their impact on the financial sector. Her remarks underscore the Federal Reserve's commitment to balancing regulatory oversight with the practical needs of banks in the United States.

data.title: Starknet: STRK20 is scheduled to launch its mainnet at the end of April and its testnet next week.
data.description: Ethereum Layer 2 network Starknet announced on the X platform that its STRK20 testnet will launch next week, with the mainnet planned for release at the end of April. STRK20 is designed to support the issuance of privacy stablecoins and other privacy assets while also meeting compliance requirements. It aims to provide token-level privacy for assets on Starknet, keeping transaction amounts and account balances confidential and compatible with DeFi applications.

data.title: After a five-month hiatus, a whale withdrew 2.265 million ASTER tokens from Binance, worth $1.54 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale withdrew 2.265 million ASTER tokens from Binance after five months of inactivity, worth $1.54 million, and then deposited them into Aster DEX.
